Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me analyze this event log carefully The task is to identify activities that appear multiple times within the same case and rename them to reflect their different contextual meanings

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First Ill look at each case individually to see which activities repeat Case C001 Verify Documents appears twice Case C002 Call Customer appears twice Case C003 Verify Documents appears twice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Here Verify Documents appears twice The first time its checking identity documents initially and the second time its re checking after the customer submitted additional documents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Here Call Customer appears twice The first call is to clarify an account discrepancy and the second is a follow up to confirm adjustments

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Here Verify Documents appears twice The first is physical verification of ID at the branch and the second is re verification after an account adjustment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Activities that appear multiple times within at least one case Verify Documents in C001 and C003 Call Customer in C002

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The task specifically asks about activities that appear multiple times within the same case So the activities to rename are Verify Documents and Call Customer

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Possible renames Initial Document Verification or Verify Initial Documents Reverify Documents or Verify Updated Documents or Verify Additional Documents

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The second verification always happens after some change to the documents or related information

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: First verification Initial Document Verification Second verification in C001 Verify Additional Documents Second verification in C003 Verify Documents After Adjustment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For C002 First Call Customer Clarification Call to Customer Second Call Customer Confirmation Call to Customer

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: To keep it consistent maybe First occurrence in any case Initial Document Verification Subsequent occurrences Reverify Documents

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait the task is to rename activities that appear multiple times within the same case So we should only rename the repeating activities within each case where they repeat

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Other instances can stay as is but that might look inconsistent Maybe better to rename all instances of these activities with their contextual names

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Given that and since the goal is to make the activity names reflect their contextual differences its better to rename all instances of these activities with their contextual names

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now lets check if this makes sense and disambiguates all repeating activities within cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Verify Documents first occurrence in each case is the initial verification of documents hence renamed to Initial Document Verification In C001 the second verification occurs after the customer submits additional documents so Verify Additional Documents In C003 the second verification happens after an account adjustment hence Verify Documents After Adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Call Customer in C001 is to request missing documents hence Request Missing Documents Call In C002 the first call is to clarify an account discrepancy and the second is to confirm adjustments In C003 the call is to have the customer return to the branch hence Customer Recall Call

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This renaming ensures that each activity name reflects its specific role in the process flow disambiguating instances that previously shared the same name but had different contexts
